translations.idiomatic: Feudal lord's audience kit.

sentences.zh: 天子負斧扆，曰：『伯父實來，余一人嘉之。

translations.literal: The Son of Heaven leans on axe-backed screen and says: 'Uncle truly came—I alone rejoice.'
translations.idiomatic: King welcomes ally by axe-screen.

sentences.zh: 』奉束帛匹馬，卓上九馬隨之，奠幣再拜。

translations.literal: Presents bundled silk and one horse—nine horses following on the dais—lays silk and bows twice.
translations.idiomatic: Gift protocol at audience.

sentences.zh: 侯氏降，天子辭於侯氏曰：『伯父無事，歸寧乃邦。

translations.literal: The lord descends; the Son of Heaven declines to the lord: 'Uncle has no further task—return home and comfort your state.'
translations.idiomatic: Dismissal formula for feudal lord.

sentences.zh: 』侯氏再拜稽首而出」也。

translations.literal: The lord bows twice touching forehead and withdraws.'"
translations.idiomatic: Exit obeisance.

sentences.zh: 詩魯頌之文也。

translations.literal: Text from the Lu hymn of the Odes.
translations.idiomatic: Lu song citation.

sentences.zh: 叔父謂周公也。

translations.literal: Uncle refers to the Duke of Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Shu refers to Zhou Gong.

sentences.zh: 建元子謂封伯禽也。

translations.literal: Establishing Yuanzi means enfeoffing Boqin.
translations.idiomatic: Jian yuan zi = invest Boqin.

sentences.zh: 見前書王莽傳。

translations.literal: See the Former Han biography of Wang Mang.
translations.idiomatic: Cross-ref. Wang Mang zhuan.

sentences.zh: 贊謂贊者不唱其名。

translations.literal: Zan means the usher did not announce his name aloud.
translations.idiomatic: Zan: silent herald.

sentences.zh: 左傳隨武子之辭也。

translations.literal: Words of Sui Wuzi in the Zuo Commentary.
translations.idiomatic: Zuo: Sui Hui's formula.

sentences.zh: 等威，威儀有等差也。

translations.literal: Deng wei means graded ritual dignity.
translations.idiomatic: Ranked ceremonial awe.

sentences.zh: 踧踖，謙讓貌也。

translations.literal: Cuji means a deferential bearing.
translations.idiomatic: Cuji: cramped humble posture.

sentences.zh: 飲酎，解見章紀。

translations.literal: Pouring zhōu wine—explanation appears in Emperor Zhang's annals.
translations.idiomatic: Zhōu libation—see Zhang ji.

sentences.zh: 大鴻臚奏王歸國，小黃門受詔者。

translations.literal: Grand herald memorializes the king's return to state; junior yellow gate receives edict.
translations.idiomatic: Honglu vs. eunuch courier.

sentences.zh: 率，循也。

translations.literal: Shuai means follow.
translations.idiomatic: Shuai: 'to follow.'

sentences.zh: 越，違也。

translations.literal: Yue means transgress.
translations.idiomatic: Yue: 'to violate.'

sentences.zh: 傅音敷。

translations.literal: Fu is pronounced fu.
translations.idiomatic: Fu read like敷 distribute.

sentences.zh: 敷，布也。

translations.literal: Fu means spread.
translations.idiomatic: Fu: 'to publish,' 'spread abroad.'

sentences.zh: 書曰：「克慎明德，敷聞在下。」

translations.literal: The Document says: 'He was able to revere bright virtue, and his glory spread below.'
translations.idiomatic: Shang shu: virtue and renown among the people.

sentences.zh: 俾，使也。

translations.literal: Bi means 'to make,' 'to let.'
translations.idiomatic: Bi: 'cause to.'

sentences.zh: 屏，蔽也。

translations.literal: Bing means 'to screen off.'
translations.idiomatic: Bing: 'to shield.'

sentences.zh: 左氏傳曰「昊天不吊，不憖遺老，俾屏余一人，殺殺余在疚」也。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition says: 'Vast heaven does not pity; it is not kind to leave the old; it had me stand alone, desolate, in my grief.'
translations.idiomatic: Zuo: heaven's indifference to the aged king.

sentences.zh: 離，被也。

translations.literal: Li means 'to suffer,' 'to undergo.'
translations.idiomatic: Li: 'to suffer (calamity).'

sentences.zh: 言非憲王誰更被蒙此恩也。

translations.literal: It means that except for King Xian who else would have received this favor.
translations.idiomatic: No one but King Xian deserved such grace.

sentences.zh: 陵在今鄲州東峗山南。

translations.literal: The tumulus stands today south of Mount Wei on the east of Danzhou.
translations.idiomatic: Mound south of Mount Wei, east Danzhou.

sentences.zh: 峗音魚委反。

translations.literal: Wei is read wei, second + yuwei fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Pronounce 峗 as wei (魚委).

sentences.zh: 憂累既近，所喪必大。

translations.literal: When worry's weight draws near, the loss must be great.
translations.idiomatic: Grief at hand foretells great loss.

sentences.zh: 言其本志然也。

translations.literal: It says this was his original intent.
translations.idiomatic: This was his true wish.

sentences.zh: 遜，讓也。

translations.literal: Xun means 'to yield.'
translations.idiomatic: Xun: 'yield,' 'defer.'

sentences.zh: 左傳*

translations.literal: Zuo Commentary*
translations.idiomatic: Lacuna after Zuo zhuan.

translations.literal: (yue)
translations.idiomatic: Superfluous yue before quotation.

sentences.zh: *晉大夫士蒍之辭也。

translations.literal: *Words of Jin grandee Shi Wei.
translations.idiomatic: Completes broken quotation attribution.

sentences.zh: 吳太伯，周太王之長子，讓其弟季歷，因適吳、越採藥，大王沒而不反，事見史記也。

translations.literal: Wu Taibo—eldest son of Grand King Zhou—yielded to younger brother Jili, then went to Wu and Yue gathering herbs; Grand King died and he did not return—the story appears in the Records.
translations.idiomatic: Taibo's flight to Wu—see Shiji.

sentences.zh: 亢父、樊並屬東平國。

translations.literal: Kangfu and Fan both belonged to Dongping kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: Kangfu and Fan in Dongping.

sentences.zh: 亢父故城在今兗州任城縣南。

translations.literal: Kangfu's old town lies south of Rencheng county in Yan prefecture today.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Kangfu south of Rencheng.

sentences.zh: 樊故城在今瑕丘縣西南也。

translations.literal: Fan's old town lies southwest of Xiaqiu county today.
translations.idiomatic: Ancient Fan southwest of Xiaqiu.

sentences.zh: 杜預注左傳曰：「今丹水縣北有三戶亭。

translations.literal: Du Yu's Zuo Commentary gloss: 'North of Danshui county is Three Households pavilion.'
translations.idiomatic: Du Yu: Three Households pavilion north of Danshui.

sentences.zh: 」故城在今鄧州內鄉縣西南也。

translations.literal: The old town lies southwest of Neixiang county in Dengzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Site southwest of Neixiang.

sentences.zh: 長平故城在今陳州宛丘縣西北，西華故城在今溵水縣西北，新陽故城在今豫州真陽西南，扶樂故城在今陳州太康縣北也。

translations.literal: Changping's old town northwest of Wanqiu in Chenzhou today; Xihua's northwest of Yinshui today; Xinyang's southwest of Zhenyang in Yuzhou today; Fule's north of Taikang county in Chenzhou today.
translations.idiomatic: Four Han county seats located relative to Song-era towns.

sentences.zh: 淮南厲王長，高帝子，文帝時反，被遷於蜀而死也。

translations.literal: The fierce king of Huainan, Liu Chang—Emperor Gao's son—rebelled under Emperor Wen and was banished to Shu where he died.
translations.idiomatic: Huainan king Chang—banished and died in Shu.

sentences.zh: 公羊傳曰：「君親無將，將而必誅。

translations.literal: The Gongyang Commentary says: 'Toward ruler or parent there must be no intent of usurpation—if there is intent, execution follows.'
translations.idiomatic: Gongyang: no treason against lord or father.

sentences.zh: 」前書曰：「大逆無道，父母、妻子、同產無少長皆棄市。」

translations.literal: Former Han says: 'Great treason without the Way—parents, wives, children, full siblings of any age—all executed in the market.'
translations.idiomatic: Hanshu penalty for great treason.

sentences.zh: 愆，過也。

translations.literal: Qian means fault.
translations.idiomatic: Qian: 'fault,' 'offense.'

sentences.zh: 反而不誅，先帝之過，故言為王受過也。

translations.literal: To rebel yet not execute—former emperors' fault—therefore it says the king bore the blame.
translations.idiomatic: Past leniency means the king paid for prior mercy.

sentences.zh: 隱害謂陰害於人也。

translations.literal: Yinhai means harming others in secret.
translations.idiomatic: Yinhai: covert injury.

sentences.zh: 方底囊，所以盛書也。

translations.literal: Fangdi sack—used to hold documents.
translations.idiomatic: Square-bottom bag for tablets.

sentences.zh: 前書曰：「綠綈方底。」

translations.literal: Former Han says: 'Green silk square bottom.'
translations.idiomatic: Hanshu green-taffeta document case.

sentences.zh: 太后，郭后也。

translations.literal: Empress dowager is Empress Guo.
translations.idiomatic: The dowager is Lady Guo.

sentences.zh: 職，常也。

translations.literal: Zhi means constant.
translations.idiomatic: Zhi: 'regular office.'

sentences.zh: 失其常位，別遷北宮。

translations.literal: Having lost her proper station she was moved separately to the Northern Palace.
translations.idiomatic: Demoted to Northern Palace quarters.

sentences.zh: 封之於魯。

translations.literal: He was enfeoffed in Lu.
translations.idiomatic: Investiture at Lu.

sentences.zh: 以便宜之事而有非者，當即行之，勿拘常制也。

translations.literal: When expedient matters arise that violate norms, carry them out at once—do not cling to routine.
translations.idiomatic: Act on discretion—break routine if needed.

sentences.zh: 累息猶疊息也。

translations.literal: Leixi is like stacked breaths—sigh upon sigh.
translations.idiomatic: Leixi: layered sighs.

sentences.zh: 喜事猶好事也。

translations.literal: Xishi is like haoshi—fond of affairs.
translations.idiomatic: Xishi: eager for business.

sentences.zh: 喜音許氣反。

translations.literal: Xi is read xi, xu + qi fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Xi read xuqi cut.

translations.literal: An asterisk marks editorial lacuna before the title fragment.
translations.idiomatic: Manuscript gap before the treatise name.

translations.literal: (Hong)
translations.idiomatic: Hong syllable—variant for Hongfan.

sentences.zh: **[洪]*范五行傳曰：「太白，少陰之星，以己未為界，不得經天而行。

translations.literal: **[Hong]*fan Five Phases treatise says: 'Tai bai—greater yin star—takes jiwei as limit—it must not transit the zenith.'
translations.idiomatic: Hongfan opens Venus omen.

sentences.zh: 太白經天而行為不臣。

translations.literal: Venus transiting the zenith means disloyal ministers.
translations.idiomatic: Venus overhead = ministerial treason.

sentences.zh: 」今至午，是為經天也。

translations.literal: ' Now reaching noon—that counts as transiting the zenith.
translations.idiomatic: At noon—zenith crossing.

sentences.zh: 天官書曰「心前星，太子之位」也。

translations.literal: Heavenly Offices treatise says 'the star before the heart—heir's seat.'
translations.idiomatic: Tian guan: star before heart = heir.

sentences.zh: 韓詩外傳曰：「昔者楚熊渠子夜行，見寢石，以為伏虎，彎弓而射之，沒金飲羽。

translations.literal: Han shi waizhuan says: 'Ancient Chu archer Xiong Quzi traveled by night, saw a sleeping stone, thought it a crouching tiger—bent his bow and shot—iron point vanished into feathers.'
translations.idiomatic: Xiong Quzi shoots stone tiger.

sentences.zh: 下視，知其石也，因復射之，矢摧無跡。

translations.literal: Looking down he knew it was stone—shot again—the arrow shattered leaving no mark.
translations.idiomatic: Second shot proves sincerity.

sentences.zh: 熊渠子見其誠心而金石為之開，而況人乎。」

translations.literal: When Quzi showed such sincerity metal and stone split open—how much more men.'
translations.idiomatic: Sincerity moves stone.

sentences.zh: 秋霜，肅殺於物。

translations.literal: Autumn frost—severity toward things.
translations.idiomatic: Autumn frost slays growth.

sentences.zh: 檻羊，受制於人。

translations.literal: Ram in pen—controlled by others.
translations.idiomatic: Penned sheep—under another's power.

sentences.zh: 陛下即光武也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty means Guangwu.
translations.idiomatic: Addresses Emperor Guangwu.

sentences.zh: 扶蘇，秦始皇之太子。

translations.literal: Fusu—crown prince of First Emperor of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Fusu, Qin heir.

sentences.zh: 將閭，庶子也。

translations.literal: Jianglu—byborn son.
translations.idiomatic: Jianglu was a younger son.

sentences.zh: 扶蘇以數諫始皇，使與蒙恬守北邊。

translations.literal: Fusu often remonstrated First Emperor—was sent with Meng Tian to guard the north.
translations.idiomatic: Fusu exiled north with Meng Tian.

sentences.zh: 始皇死於沙丘，少子胡亥詐立，賜扶蘇死。

translations.literal: First Emperor died at Shaqiu—younger son Hu Hai forged succession—ordered Fusu to die.
translations.idiomatic: Hu Hai's forged edict kills Fusu.

sentences.zh: 將閭昆弟三人囚於內宮。

translations.literal: The three Jianglu brothers were imprisoned in the inner palace.
translations.idiomatic: Three princes held in inner palace.

sentences.zh: 胡亥使謂將閭曰：「公子不臣，罪當死。

translations.literal: Hu Hai's envoy told Jianglu: 'Young lord, you failed your ministerial duty—death is fitting.'
translations.idiomatic: Hu Hai charges treason.

sentences.zh: 」將閭乃仰天而大呼天者三，曰：「天乎！

translations.literal: Jianglu then cried to heaven three great cries, saying: 'O Heaven!'
translations.idiomatic: Jianglu's triple cry to heaven.

sentences.zh: 吾無罪。

translations.literal: ' I am without crime.'
translations.idiomatic: Protests innocence.

sentences.zh: 」昆弟三人皆流涕，伏劒自殺。

translations.literal: ' All three brothers wept and fell on their swords.
translations.idiomatic: Triple suicide.

sentences.zh: 事見史記。

translations.literal: The tale appears in the Records.
translations.idiomatic: See Shiji.

sentences.zh: 漢官儀：「騶騎，王家名官騎。」

translations.literal: Han office regulations: 'Zouqi—royal household calls them official riders.'
translations.idiomatic: Zouqi = royal mounted escort.

sentences.zh: 穀梁傳曰，公會齊侯於頰谷，齊人鼓噪，欲以執魯君。

translations.literal: The Guliang Commentary says the duke met Marquis of Qi at Jiagu—Qi men drummed and clamored meaning to seize the Lu lord.
translations.idiomatic: Guliang: Jiagu crisis.

sentences.zh: 孔子歷階而上，命司馬止之。

translations.literal: Confucius mounted the steps and ordered the marshal to stop them.
translations.idiomatic: Kongzi stops Qi by marshal.

sentences.zh: 左氏傳「頰谷」作「夾谷」。

translations.literal: The Zuo Tradition has 夾谷 (Jia Valley) where the Guliang text reads 頰谷 (Jia Valley).
translations.idiomatic: Zuo zhuan spells the conference site 夾谷; Guliang uses 頰谷 for the same place.

sentences.zh: 娖音楚角反。

translations.literal: Chuo is read chuo, chu + jue fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Chuo pronunciation.

sentences.zh: 稱娖猶齊整也。

translations.literal: Said chuo means neat and aligned.
translations.idiomatic: Chuo: orderly.

sentences.zh: 行音胡郎反。

translations.literal: Hang is read hang, hu + lang fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Hang read hulang.

sentences.zh: 司馬相如子虛之文。

translations.literal: From Sima Xiangru's Zixu rhapsody.
translations.idiomatic: Zixu fu citation.

sentences.zh: 安險屬中山郡。

translations.literal: Anxian belonged to Zhongshan commandery.
translations.idiomatic: Anxian in Zhongshan.

sentences.zh: 爾雅曰「女子之子為出」也。

translations.literal: The Erya says 'a woman's child is called chu.'
translations.idiomatic: Erya chu gloss.

sentences.zh: 墓前開道，建石柱以為標，謂之神道。

translations.literal: They opened a road before the tomb and erected stone pillars as markers—called spirit way.
translations.idiomatic: Shendao: avenue with pillars before tomb.

sentences.zh: 黃腸，柏木黃心。

translations.literal: Huangchang—cypress beams with yellow heartwood.
translations.idiomatic: Huangchang: cypress core planks.

sentences.zh: 蓋縣故城在今沂州沂水縣西北。

translations.literal: Gai county's old town northwest of Yishui county in Yi prefecture today.
translations.idiomatic: Gai northwest of Yishui.

sentences.zh: 南武陽縣故城在今沂州費縣西，又華縣故城在費縣東北也。

translations.literal: South Wuyang's old town west of Fei county in Yi prefecture today; Hua's old town northeast of Fei.
translations.idiomatic: South Wuyang and Hua near Fei.

sentences.zh: 昌陽，今萊州縣也，故城在今聞登縣西南。

translations.literal: Changyang is modern Laizhou county; old town southwest of Wendeng county today.
translations.idiomatic: Changyang / Wendeng locale.

sentences.zh: 盧鄉故城今昌陽縣西北。

translations.literal: Luxiang's old town northwest of Changyang county today.
translations.idiomatic: Luxiang northwest of Changyang.

sentences.zh: 東牟故城在聞登縣西北也。

translations.literal: East Mou's old town northwest of Wendeng county today.
translations.idiomatic: East Mou northwest of Wendeng.

sentences.zh: 壁帶，壁中之橫木也，以金銀為釭，飾其上。

translations.literal: Wall belt—horizontal timber in the wall—gold and silver bosses ornament it.
translations.idiomatic: Carved wall belt with metal bosses.

sentences.zh: 華縣、蓋縣、南武陽屬泰山郡，厚丘屬東海郡，贛榆屬琅邪郡。

translations.literal: Hua, Gai, and South Wuyang belonged to Mount Tai commandery; Houqiu to Donghai; Ganyu to Langye.
translations.idiomatic: County jurisdictions listed.

sentences.zh: 開陽，縣，屬東海郡，故城在今沂州臨沂縣北。

translations.literal: Kaiyang—county—belonged to Donghai commandery; old town north of Linyi county in Yi prefecture today.
translations.idiomatic: Kaiyang north of Linyi.

sentences.zh: 陽都，縣，屬城陽國，故城在今沂州承縣南。

translations.literal: Yangdu—county—belonged to Chengyang kingdom; old town south of Cheng county in Yi prefecture today.
translations.idiomatic: Yangdu south of Cheng.

sentences.zh: 承音常證反。

translations.literal: Cheng is read cheng, chang + zheng fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Cheng reading.

sentences.zh: 尊音祖本反。

translations.literal: Zun is read zun, zu + ben fanqie.
translations.idiomatic: Zun pronunciation.

sentences.zh: 禮記曰：「恭敬撙節。

translations.literal: The Record of Rites says: 'Reverent and respectful, restrained and measured.'
translations.idiomatic: Liji on deferential conduct.

sentences.zh: 」鄭玄注云：「撙，趨也。」

translations.literal: Zheng Xuan notes: 'Zun means to hurry.'
translations.idiomatic: Zheng: zun = hasten.

sentences.zh: 二王早終，名聞未著也。

translations.literal: The two kings died young—repute never rose.
translations.idiomatic: Two princes died young and obscure.
